questions.scenario: A request asks for `Contributor` on the production subscription so `devpolaris-orders-api` can write monthly exports to one storage account. What should the reviewer prefer?

options.text: A storage data role scoped to the specific storage account, if that satisfies the export write path.
Contributor on the subscription because it is easier to reuse later.
Owner on the resource group because exports are production data.
No RBAC assignment because managed identities automatically get storage access.

questions.explanation: Least privilege starts with the action and target resource. Broad roles should not be used when a narrower data-plane role and scope fit the job.

questions.scenario: Before creating a role assignment, which facts should be clear?

options.text: Which principal will receive access.
Which role is needed.
Which scope should contain the access.
Which portal color theme the reviewer uses.

questions.explanation: The three RBAC facts are principal, role, and scope. Everything else is secondary to those facts.

questions.scenario: A role assignment gives `Reader` at the resource group scope, but the app still cannot read a Key Vault secret. What is the likely issue?

options.text: Reader lets the identity view management metadata, but it does not grant secret read access.
Reader is too narrow because it only works at subscription scope.
The app needs DNS access before it can use RBAC.
The role assignment must be attached to a secret value instead of a principal.

questions.explanation: Role names matter. Management-plane reader access is not the same as permission to read secret values.

questions.scenario: The team assigns Key Vault access at the vault scope. Later, the app needs to write Blob Storage exports. What is the right review habit?

options.text: Create a separate assignment for the storage action at the storage scope instead of expanding the Key Vault assignment.
Change the Key Vault role to Contributor so it can cover more resources.
Grant subscription Owner to avoid repeated reviews.
Move Blob Storage into the vault because the app already has vault access.

questions.explanation: Different jobs can require different roles and scopes. Keep assignments tied to the specific action and resource family.

questions.scenario: A failed deploy shows `AuthorizationFailed` for `Microsoft.App/containerApps/write` at `rg-devpolaris-orders-prod`. Which access path should be checked first?

options.text: The deployment caller's role assignment at the resource group or container app scope.
The runtime app identity's Key Vault secret role.
The storage blob data role used by monthly exports.
The public DNS record for the API.

questions.explanation: The error names a management action on Container Apps. That belongs to the deployment caller, not the runtime identity reading secrets.
